tool.name: terminal_execute
tool.description: Execute a bash command in a persistent terminal session. Maintains state (environment variables, current directory, running processes) between commands.

parameter.name: command
parameter.description: Bash command to execute. Can be empty to check output of running commands.

parameter.name: is_input
parameter.description: If true, send as input to a running process. If false (default), execute as new command.

parameter.name: timeout
parameter.description: Timeout in seconds (max 60). Default: 30. On timeout, command keeps running and returns status 'running'.

parameter.name: terminal_id
parameter.description: Terminal session identifier. Default: "default". Use different IDs for multiple concurrent sessions.

parameter.name: no_enter
parameter.description: If true, do not add Enter after the command. Useful for interactive prompts or Vim.

parameter.name: trusted_command
parameter.description: If true, bypass command safety checks. Default false.

returns.description: Content (output), exit_code, command, terminal_id, status (completed or running), and working_dir.
